---
title: "Unit 2b — Probability & Estimation Foundations (II)"
subtitle: "Matrix calculus (Jacobian identities) · Gaussian independence · MLE unbiasedness · Beta moments"
format:
  typst:
    papersize: us-letter
    margin: { x: 1.25in, y: 1in }
---

# 1. Context & purpose

The matrix-calculus identities and distributional results the rest of the course leans on.
**Part A** derives the gradient identities **applied** in Unit 1 (the normal equation) and used
again for the Newton–Raphson Hessian (Unit 5). **Part B** establishes Gaussian independence,
proves the Unit-2a MLE is unbiased, and derives the Beta moments that power the conjugate priors
of Unit 4.

# 2. Learning objectives

| | Objective | Bloom | Bundle · spec |
|---|-----------|-------|----------------|
| **O1** | Derive the Jacobian identities `∂w/∂w=I`, `∂/∂wᵀ f=(∂f/∂w)ᵀ`, `∂/∂wᵀ(Cw)=C` | Apply/Analyze | Correctness · A1–A3 |
| **O2** | Show a diagonal-covariance Gaussian factorizes into independent univariate Gaussians | Analyze | Correctness · B1 |
| **O3** | Prove the Bernoulli MLE `r̂` is unbiased | Apply | Correctness · B2 |
| **O4** | Derive the Beta mean and variance | Analyze | Correctness · B3–B4 |
| **O5** | Interpret: where symmetry matters; unbiasedness vs. variance; Beta as a prior | Evaluate | Interpretation · I1–I3 |

# 3. The task

Derive all seven results. **Any valid route earns full credit** (explicit components or the
Kronecker-delta form for Part A; for B4, `var = E[r²] − (E[r])²`).

# 4. What you may / may not use

Standard matrix calculus and the gamma identity `Γ(n+1)=nΓ(n)`. State your assumptions
(symmetry of `C` in A3; i.i.d. and `E[xₙ]=r` in B2; `α,β>0` in B3–B4).

# 5. How you'll be assessed

Specifications grading (`specs.md`) — **this unit is written-only, so the entire Correctness /
gateway bundle is graded per-step by a human** (each step met at *Proficient+*); same bundle
structure as Unit 1, all `Verified by: human`. Interpretation (I1–I3, CERL) and Process
(notation/assumptions, legibility) are separate bundles. Any unmet bundle → one
revise-and-resubmit. *No autograder — the shared `classroom.yml` skips pytest for written units.*

---

## Part A — Matrix-calculus (Jacobian) identities

> These are the identities **Unit 1 applied** to obtain the normal equation
> `ŵ=(XᵀX)⁻¹Xᵀt`; here we derive them generally. A3 reappears in **Unit 5** as the Hessian term.

**A1.** `∂w/∂w = I`. **A2.** for scalar `f`, `∂/∂wᵀ f = (∂f/∂w)ᵀ`. **A3.** `∂/∂wᵀ(Cw)=C`.

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Your derivations (A1–A3):**  `%%% YOUR ANSWER HERE %%%`
:::

<!-- SOLUTION START -->
::: {.callout-tip title="Reference derivation (instructor)"}
**A1.** The $(i,j)$ entry of $\partial\mathbf{w}/\partial\mathbf{w}$ is
$\partial w_i/\partial w_j=\delta_{ij}$, i.e. $\mathbf{I}$.

**A2.** $\partial/\partial\mathbf{w}^\top f=[\partial f/\partial w_1,\dots,\partial f/\partial w_D]$
is a **row** vector; $\partial f/\partial\mathbf{w}$ is the **column** of the same partials, so one
is the transpose of the other.

**A3.** $(\mathbf{Cw})_i=\sum_j C_{ij}w_j$, so
$\partial (\mathbf{Cw})_i/\partial w_k=\sum_j C_{ij}\delta_{jk}=C_{ik}$; assembling over $(i,k)$
gives $\boxed{\;\partial/\partial\mathbf{w}^\top(\mathbf{Cw})=\mathbf{C}\;}$.
:::
<!-- SOLUTION END -->

## Part B — Gaussian independence, MLE unbiasedness, Beta moments

**B1.** A diagonal $\boldsymbol\Sigma=\mathrm{diag}(\sigma_1^2,\dots,\sigma_D^2)$ makes the
$D$-variate Gaussian a product of independent univariate Gaussians.
**B2.** The Bernoulli MLE $\hat r=\frac1N\sum_n x_n$ (Unit 2a) is unbiased.
**B3/B4.** The Beta$(\alpha,\beta)$ mean and variance.

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Your derivations (B1–B4):**  `%%% YOUR ANSWER HERE %%%`
:::

<!-- SOLUTION START -->
::: {.callout-tip title="Reference derivation (instructor)"}
**B1.** $|\boldsymbol\Sigma|=\prod_d\sigma_d^2$ and $\boldsymbol\Sigma^{-1}=\mathrm{diag}(1/\sigma_d^2)$,
so the quadratic form is $\sum_d (x_d-\mu_d)^2/\sigma_d^2$ and (exp of a sum = product of exps)
$p(\mathbf{w})=\prod_{d=1}^D \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi\sigma_d^2}}\exp\!\Big(-\frac{(x_d-\mu_d)^2}{2\sigma_d^2}\Big)$
— a product of univariate Gaussians ⇒ independence.

**B2.** $\mathbb{E}[\hat r]=\mathbb{E}\big[\tfrac1N\sum_n x_n\big]=\tfrac1N\sum_n\mathbb{E}[x_n]
=\tfrac1N\,(Nr)=r$ (linearity; $\mathbb{E}[x_n]=r$). Unbiased.

**B3.** $\mathbb{E}[r]=\int_0^1 r\,\frac{\Gamma(\alpha+\beta)}{\Gamma(\alpha)\Gamma(\beta)}
r^{\alpha-1}(1-r)^{\beta-1}dr=\frac{\Gamma(\alpha+\beta)}{\Gamma(\alpha)\Gamma(\beta)}
\frac{\Gamma(\alpha+1)\Gamma(\beta)}{\Gamma(\alpha+\beta+1)}=\boxed{\dfrac{\alpha}{\alpha+\beta}}$
(using $\Gamma(n+1)=n\Gamma(n)$).

**B4.** Similarly $\mathbb{E}[r^2]=\dfrac{\alpha(\alpha+1)}{(\alpha+\beta)(\alpha+\beta+1)}$, so
$\mathrm{var}(r)=\mathbb{E}[r^2]-(\mathbb{E}[r])^2=\boxed{\dfrac{\alpha\beta}{(\alpha+\beta)^2(\alpha+\beta+1)}}.$
:::
<!-- SOLUTION END -->

> **Through-lines.** A1–A3 ↔ Unit 1 (normal equation) and Unit 5 (Newton Hessian). B2 builds on
> the Unit-2a MLE. B3–B4 are the moments of the **Beta** — the conjugate prior for a probability
> in **Unit 4** (and the posterior of the coin game).

# 6. Required interpretation (CERL)

*LLM may pre-grade; human confirms and is final.*

- **I1.** Why does A3 need `C` **symmetric** in the squared-form `∂/∂wᵀ(wᵀCw)` case, and where are
  these identities used downstream?
- **I2.** What does **unbiasedness** guarantee — and what does it *not* (variance, consistency)?
- **I3.** Read `α,β` as prior pseudo-counts: what happens to the Beta mean/variance as `α+β→∞`,
  and why does that make Beta a natural **prior** for a probability?

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Your answers (I1–I3):**  `%%% YOUR ANSWER HERE %%%`
:::

# 7. Going further (optional)

State `∂/∂wᵀ(wᵀCw)=wᵀ(C+Cᵀ)` for general `C` and confirm it reduces to `2wᵀC` when `C=Cᵀ`.

# 8. Submission & reproducibility

Written unit — render with `quarto render unit2b.qmd` (Typst, no TeX); submit the derivations.
Keep notation consistent and state every assumption.
